title: Handbook for Systematic Reviews in International Relations
author: Saaida, Mohammed
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In a world of ever-growing research on international relations, navigating the complex web of findings can be a daunting task. The Handbook for Systematic Reviews in International Relations offers a much-needed guide for scholars seeking to synthesize evidence and produce robust analyses. This comprehensive volume equips researchers with the tools and best practices to conduct systematic reviews, the gold standard for evaluating existing literature. By following a clear and replicable methodology, researchers can ensure the transparency, rigor, and reliability of their reviews, ultimately shaping a more informed understanding of critical issues in the field. This handbook goes beyond simply outlining steps. It delves into the intricacies of formulating research questions, crafting effective search strategies across diverse databases, and critically appraising the quality of included studies. By addressing both quantitative and qualitative research methods, the Handbook caters to the full spectrum of research designs employed in international relations. With its focus on reducing bias and ensuring replicability, this book empowers researchers to become active participants in building a more cumulative and trustworthy body of knowledge within the discipline.
